page: Vultr vbm-112c-2048gb-8-h100-gpu is a bare-metal server configured for high-density parallel computing and artificial intelligence workloads. The system features 224 dedicated physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8480+ processors on the x86_64 architecture, operating at a base frequency of 2.0 GHz with a single thread per core. This massive CPU allocation is paired with 2015.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 9.0 GB of RAM per core to support data-intensive operations.

For acceleration, the server integrates eight NVIDIA H100 Hopper GPUs, delivering a combined 640 GB of high-bandwidth VRAM. Local storage is provided by a 1920 GB NVMe SSD, ensuring fast read and write speeds for operating systems and local datasets. The server also includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this bare-metal configuration represents a high-density hardware profile designed to maximize resource utilization for enterprise-scale tasks. By deploying on bare metal, users avoid virtualization overhead, optimizing the performance-to-resource ratio for workloads such as large language model training, deep learning, complex scientific simulations, and massive parallel data processing.

Local storage is supported by a 1920 GB NVMe SSD, and the configuration includes one public IPv4 address. This bare-metal architecture eliminates virtualization overhead, offering high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for large-scale deployments. It is engineered for workloads such as deep learning, large language model training, and heavy data science jobs.
